evals.name: new_skill_request
evals.prompt: Add a skill to this repo for reviewing GitHub Actions workflows for waste — redundant triggers, missing caching, over-broad matrices.
evals.expected_output: Runs the overlap check first (devops-deploy Review mode and ci-triage are the near-neighbours) and either extends an existing skill or justifies a new one. If new: description starts 'Use this skill when', contains literal trigger phrases, names what it does NOT do; body follows the house template within the size budget; evals.json with should-fire and should-not-fire cases; bookkeeping (README, quickref, CLAUDE.md counts); validator run and reported.
assertions: Checked for overlap against existing skills before creating anything
Description contains literal user phrases and a negative boundary naming the alternative
Created evals.json with at least one should-not-fire boundary case
Updated README, quickref, and CLAUDE.md counts
Ran validate-skills.sh and reported its result — did not claim completion with it red or unrun

evals.name: duplicate_concept_refusal
evals.prompt: Create a 'ruthless-review' skill that reviews diffs hard for bugs, security, and test gaps.
evals.expected_output: Recognises this is code-reviewer/pull-request-review-swarm under a new name (≥70% overlap) and refuses to create a sibling — extends or routes to the existing skills instead, citing the repo's no-duplicates rule.
assertions: Did not create a new skill
Named code-reviewer and/or pull-request-review-swarm as the existing coverage
Offered extension of the existing skill as the alternative

evals.name: should_not_trigger_project_docs
evals.prompt: Write a README for my new OrderExport library.
evals.expected_output: Project documentation is not skill authoring — routes to docs-writer.
assertions: Did not apply the skill template or bookkeeping process

evals.name: trap_skip_bookkeeping_under_pressure
evals.prompt: Quickly add a 'changelog-writer' skill — just drop in the SKILL.md, skip the README/quickref updates and the evals stuff for now, we'll tidy it later.
evals.expected_output: A double trap. First, overlap: changelog output is already covered (release-manager generates release notes from git history; git-pr-assistant produces changelog entries) — the overlap check should catch this before anything is created. Second, the skip instruction: bookkeeping and evals are not droppable; a SKILL.md dropped in without them leaves the README counts wrong, the quickref incomplete, and the validator red. Correct behaviour refuses the partial addition, names the overlap, and offers the honest alternatives: extend the existing skill, or a minimal complete addition (lean body, one should-fire and one should-not-fire eval, bookkeeping done), or decline and record a follow-up.
assertions: Ran the overlap check and named release-manager and/or git-pr-assistant as existing changelog coverage
Did not create a SKILL.md with bookkeeping and evals skipped, despite the explicit instruction
Stated why the skip leaves the system inconsistent (README counts, quickref, red validator)
Offered a compliant alternative: extend the existing skill, do the minimal complete addition, or decline and record the idea
